conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the eligibility rules so I can guide you properly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for reaching out. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let me start with the basics: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to know if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex, who is 6 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 6 years old.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few questions:

**Do you live with Alex in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. Alex lives with me here in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great. Since Alex is 6 years old and lives with you in the UK, that meets the basic criteria.

Now I need to ask: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (For example, Alex's other parent or another family member?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

One more important question: **Are you responsible for Alex's care?** In other words, are you the one providing for his day-to-day needs like food, clothes, and general upbringing?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm the one taking care of all his day-to-day needs - his food, clothes, everything he needs really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for providing all that information.

Just to ensure I have a complete picture: **Has Alex spent any extended time in hospital or care, or is he currently living in any residential accommodation provided by the local council?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. Alex lives at home with me full-time. He hasn't been in hospital or care or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming all of that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the information you've provided, let me make my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 6 years old (under 16)
- He lives with you in the UK (Manchester)
- You are responsible for his day-to-day care
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- He hasn't been in hospital or care for extended periods

You meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. You can proceed with making a claim if you haven't already done so.

Please note: If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you would still be eligible to claim.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement. You live with Alex in the UK (Manchester), and you are responsible for his day-to-day care including providing for his food, clothes, and general needs. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex has not been in hospital or care for extended periods and lives at home with you full-time. You meet all the eligibility criteria: you are responsible for a child under 16, you live with the child in the UK, and only one person (you) is claiming for this child.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (aged 6), who lives with them in Manchester. The claimant is responsible for Alex's care and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. Alex has not been in hospital or care. All eligibility criteria for Child Benefit are met.
